entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: I know this is the place for Tax Reporting, but I've tried the posing the question in the SAP Benefits Build Work Zone and received no responses. We have implemented all of the notes and defined the minimum and maximum age eligibility in V_T7USBEN03. The calculation of the higher catch-up limit works as expected for our 401k plan and our 457 plan ONLY if the ee contribution is a fixed amount. If the ee contribution is a percentage, only our 401k plan works. Does anyone know what could be causing this to work for one plan but not for the other (457)?

entry.title: Do you want to know the key features SAP Intelligent Agriculture and how it can help agribusinesses?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing and Configuring SAP Intelligent Agriculture learning journey.

Overview
SAP Intelligent Agriculture is a cloud-native solution designed to help agribusinesses and consumer goods companies digitalize their farming processes and services. By utilizing data science and machine learning, it aids in making more informed decisions.
In this Learning Journey, you will learn how to implement and configure the solution. The topics covered include system onboarding, understanding the farm data model, and using the APIs.

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to:

  • Onboard the solution on the SAP Business Technology Platform cockpit and manage user authorizations.
  • Understand the data model and key entities.
  • Load data using the APIs.

Prerequisites

  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform 
  • Functional roles should complete Units 1 and 2.
  • Technical roles need to know Units 1 through 4 and Unit 7, but it is highly recommended that they obtain a record of achievement for the entire learning journey.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: CAP Node.js Plugins (2 of 3) - Using the REPL
entry.content: In this second of a three part series we use the cds REPL to introspect the running service, and explore the entities, elements and annotations, so that we know enough for the third part where we'll write our own plugin. See the series post CAP Node.js plugins for more details and links to all the related resources.

entry.title: Re: Account determination for interests posting in public cloud
entry.content: Thanks for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: CAP Node.js Plugins (3 of 3) - Writing our own
entry.content: In this third of a three part series, having understood how plugins work (in part 1) and learned how to use the cds REPL to explore entities, elements and annotations of a running service (in part 2), we now know enough to write our own plugin which we'll do in this last part. See the series post CAP Node.js plugins for more details and links to all the related resources.

entry.title: Re: Map Operation Activity to specific Recourse in SAP DMC

Work Center POD shows all Operations planned for the selected Work Center. You can restrict Operations for specific Resources using 'Resource Type' mapping, just search SAP Help for SAP Digital Manufacturing for "Resource Types" for more details.

entry.title: Streamlining SAP Operations and Monitoring with SSM-SAP and Streamlit
entry.content:
Gartner predicts Cloud Will Become a Business Necessity by 2028. Migrating SAP to cloud is a strategic decision for many organizations.

AWS, a pioneer in cloud solutions, has consistently innovated new services and tools, such as the prescriptive Cloud Migration Framework, Migration Hub, and Launch Wizard. Most recently, AWS introduced a new tool: AWS Systems Manager for SAP (ssm-sap). This tool offers many features that organizations have been anticipating, including:

  • Registering and discovering SAP applications
  • Listing discovered SAP applications
  • Listing configurations of discovered SAP applications
  • Integration with AWS Backup

In this article, we will introduce the basics of ssm-sap and explore how to use it to:

  • Discover SAP systems
  • Monitor SAP systems
  • Perform basic operations such as starting and stopping SAP systems

Natively, ssm-sap is CLI-based. To visualize the operations in real time, we will use powerful web based visualization tool: Streamlit. In a sequel to this blog, we plan to provide all the codes used, and discuss more advanced topics such as performing system refreshes and auto-scaling.

Here we will explore the integration of AWS Systems Manager for SAP (SSM-SAP) with Streamlit to create a robust and user-friendly solution for managing SAP systems. By leveraging SSM-SAP's capabilities for discovery, automation, and monitoring, combined with Streamlit's interactive web application framework, we propose a solution that automates key tasks such as system discovery, visualization of system health, and execution of essential operations like starting and stopping SAP instances.

The proposed solution offers significant benefits, including:

* Simplified System Management: Streamlined processes for discovering and managing SAP systems.

* Enhanced Visibility: Real-time monitoring of system health.

* Automated Operations: Automated execution of common tasks, reducing manual effort and potential errors.

* User-Friendly Interface: An intuitive web interface for easy interaction and control.

By combining the power of SSM-SAP and Streamlit, we aim to empower SAP administrators to efficiently manage their SAP environments and improve overall system reliability.

I. Introduction to ssm-sap

AWS Systems Manager for SAP (ssm-sap) is a welcome addition to SAP management after Launch Wizard for SAP and AWS backup for HANA. It also integrates well with the other SAP facilities. If we provision SAP systems via Launch Wizard, ssm-sap registration and AWS backup are automatically configured during the process, as seen here from Launch Wizard screen.

In case that SAP servers are provisioned elsewhere, it is possible to register manually. Please refer to

  • Register your SAP HANA databases with Systems Manager for SAP
  • Register your SAP ABAP application with AWS Systems Manager for SAP

We can use ssm-sap to discover all the SAP applications, databases and their components, e.g.,

aws ssm-sap list-applications
{
"Applications": [
{
"Id": "LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA",
"DiscoveryStatus": "SUCCESS",
"Type": "HANA",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA",
"Tags": {}
},
{
"Id": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP",
"DiscoveryStatus": "SUCCESS",
"Type": "SAP_ABAP",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:SAP_ABAP/LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP",
"Tags": {}
},
{
"Id": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA",
"DiscoveryStatus": "SUCCESS",
"Type": "HANA",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA",
"Tags": {}
}
]
}

aws ssm-sap list-components --application-id LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP 
{
"Components": [
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP",
"ComponentId": "ECD-D12-newnw2",
"ComponentType": "DIALOG",
"Tags": {},
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:SAP_ABAP/LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP/COMPONENT/ECD-D12-newnw2"
},
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP",
"ComponentId": "ECD-ASCS10-newnw2",
"ComponentType": "ASCS",
"Tags": {},
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:SAP_ABAP/LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP/COMPONENT/ECD-ASCS10-newnw2"
},
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP",
"ComponentId": "ECD-ABAP",
"ComponentType": "ABAP",
"Tags": {},
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:SAP_ABAP/LaunchWizardnewnw1_ABAP/COMPONENT/ECD-ABAP"
}
]
}

aws ssm-sap list-databases
{
"Databases": [
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA",
"ComponentId": "HDB-HDB00",
"DatabaseId": "HDB",
"DatabaseType": "TENANT",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA/DB/HDB",
"Tags": {}
},
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA",
"ComponentId": "HDB-HDB00",
"DatabaseId": "SYSTEMDB",
"DatabaseType": "SYSTEM",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewhana2_HANA/DB/SYSTEMDB",
"Tags": {}
},
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA",
"ComponentId": "HDV-HDB00",
"DatabaseId": "HDV",
"DatabaseType": "TENANT",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA/DB/HDV",
"Tags": {}
},
{
"ApplicationId": "LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA",
"ComponentId": "HDV-HDB00",
"DatabaseId": "SYSTEMDB",
"DatabaseType": "SYSTEM",
"Arn": "arn:aws:ssm-sap:us-east-1:123456789012:HANA/LaunchWizardnewnw1_HANA/DB/SYSTEMDB",
"Tags": {}
}
]
}

For each component of applications and each database, we may find their running status by get-component or get-database.

Hint: attach help at end of command to get a guide online in awscli.

  aws ssm-sap get-component help

  aws ssm-sap get-database help

Here is a list of all possible statuses:

The status of the component.

  • ACTIVATED - this status has been deprecated.
  • STARTING - the component is in the process of being started.
  • STOPPED - the component is not running.
  • STOPPING - the component is in the process of being stopped.
  • RUNNING - the component is running.
  • RUNNING_WITH_ERROR - one or more child component(s) of the parent component is not running.
  • UNDEFINED - AWS Systems Manager for SAP cannot provide the component status based on the discovered information. Verify your SAP application.

Through ssm-sap, starting and stopping SAP become as easy as:

  aws ssm-sap start-application ...

  aws ssm-sap stop-application ...

As seen, ssm-sap does provide comprehensive tools to discover, monitor and operate SAP systems. Nevertheless, when there are tens or hundreds of systems, it could become a daunting task to manage and monitor all by command lines. That is where visualization tool streamlit comes to the rescue.

II. Introduction to streamlit

Streamlit is an open-source Python library that makes it easy to create and share amazing custom web apps for machine learning and data science. In just a few minutes, you can build and deploy powerful data-driven applications. Streamlit is designed to be simple and intuitive, allowing users to transform data scripts into interactive web applications without requiring extensive web development knowledge.

Key Features,

  1. Ease of Use: Streamlit allows you to create web apps with pure Python. No need for HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.
  2. Interactive Widgets: Add interactive widgets like sliders, buttons, and text inputs with minimal code.
  3. Real-Time Updates: Streamlit apps update in real-time as users interact with widgets, providing immediate feedback.
  4. Data Visualization: Integrate with popular data visualization libraries like Matplotlib, Plotly, and Altair.
  5. Deployment: Easily deploy your Streamlit apps to the web using Streamlit Sharing, Docker, or cloud services like AWS.

Please check https://docs.streamlit.io/get-started to start with streamlit.

Here is a simple example with intro.py,

 

 
import streamlit as st
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

chart_data = pd.DataFrame(
     np.random.randn(20, 3),
     columns=['a', 'b', 'c'])

st.line_chart(chart_data)
 

Once kicking off "streamlit run intro.py", we have this chart,

 

There is a whole gallery of streamlit applications in :  https://streamlit.io/gallery

III. Use streamlit to monitor and perform operations

By utilizing Streamlit, you can build an intuitive and interactive dashboard to monitor and operate SAP systems. This dashboard can call ssm-sap to perform various management tasks, providing a seamless and user-friendly interface for SAP administrators.

Initially all applications are in stopped states:

We click button Start All 

 

In a few minutes, we will see HANA starts first.

Finally, all starts.

Summary:

By combining AWS Systems Manager (SSM) with Streamlit, one can create a powerful, cost-effective solution for monitoring and managing SAP environments. This setup leverages AWS’s robust management capabilities and Streamlit’s flexible visualization tools to provide a comprehensive solution that can save time and reduce costs associated with SAP system maintenance (patching and upgrade etc) and operations.

entry.title: BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration and Application Deployment Steps to CF and on-premise
entry.content:
SAP BTP Trial Account, BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration, and Application Deployment Steps

The Importance of SAP BTP and Integration: A Robust Foundation for Digital Transformation

In the era of digital transformation, having a flexible and reliable IT infrastructure is essential for businesses to enhance efficiency and maintain a competitive edge. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a modern cloud platform designed to meet these demands, enabling businesses to accelerate innovation, optimize processes, and develop new digital solutions.

A key component of this platform is SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), which provides developers with a powerful and user-friendly development environment. With BAS, creating, developing, and deploying cloud-based applications becomes faster and more efficient.

By integrating tools like SAP Cloud Connector, businesses can seamlessly connect on-premise systems with SAP BTP, ensuring secure and compliant data exchange. This integration supports hybrid system architectures, allowing companies to leverage their existing ABAP systems while benefiting from the flexibility and scalability of the cloud.

This comprehensive guide outlines the step-by-step process for setting up SAP BTP, configuring BAS, integrating with on-premise systems, and deploying applications to both Cloud Foundry and ABAP environments. Whether you're exploring SAP BTP for the first time or aiming to streamline your development processes, this document serves as a valuable resource for navigating the complexities of modern enterprise technology.

Creating Subaccount in a BTP Trial Account and Adding Services

  1. Visit https://account.hana.ondemand.com/ to create a BTP trial account.
  2. After registering, navigate to the Trial Home page and proceed to the Trial Account section to create a subaccount.
  3. Access the subaccount you created and load the required contents via the Entitlements section.
    • First, click the Edit button.
    • Then, click the Add Service Plans button to add the necessary services.

       



Business Application Studio Setup and Required Content Installation

To set up Business Application Studio (BAS) and install the necessary content, you can follow either of these two approaches:

  1. Adding the Service to an Existing Subaccount
    If you have already created a subaccount, you can add Business Application Studio to it by selecting the service addition option. Alternatively, you can directly go to the BOOSTER menu, search for "Business App Studio," or find it in the list. From there, you can create the subaccount while setting up BAS.
  2. Setting Up the Services
    After the installation is complete, go to the Entitlements menu, click Configure Entitlements, and add the following services in the order listed:
    • SAP Build Work Zone
    • SAP Business Application Studio
    • SAP HANA Cloud
    • SAP HANA Schemas & HDI Containers
    • Mobile Services
    • Cloud Foundry Runtime/Environment
  3. Creating the Business Application Studio Instance
    Next, create the BAS instance from the Instance menu.
  4. Running Business Application Studio
    Once BAS is installed and the services are configured, start the Business Application Studio.
  5. Creating a New Workspace
    After running BAS, create a new workspace for your project.
  6. Ready to Use
    Your Business Application Studio is now ready to use.

     

This setup process ensures that all required services are added and that your BAS environment is configured and running, ready for your development work.

 

Installing and Configuring SAP Cloud Connector

After setting up Business Application Studio (BAS), if you wish to integrate it with an ABAP system that uses a VPN, you will need to create a connection point using SAP Cloud Connector. SAP Cloud Connector provides the following functionalities:

  • Secure connection between SAP BTP and on-premise systems.
  • Controls data flow between systems.
  • Ensures compliance with security policies.

You can download the SAP Cloud Connector installation file from the following link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

Installation on Windows:

  1. Double-click the installation file to start the process.
  2. Accept the license agreement.
  3. Choose the installation directory (e.g., C:\SAP\CloudConnector).
  4. Click Install and wait for the installation to complete.

Step 1: Starting the Cloud Connector

  1. Navigate to Start > SAP Cloud Connector to launch the application.
  2. It will automatically open in your default browser. Alternatively, you can manually open the URL: http://localhost:8443 .

Step 2: Logging In

  • Username: Administrator
  • Password: manage

If you have previously logged in and changed your password but forgot it, you can reset the password by editing the users.xml file located in the C:\SAP\scc20\config directory. Change the password field to the encrypted value: "3F282FCBA8933E03A65A6DC92A27DE8396961E2F". This will reset the password to manage. After saving the file, restart SAP Cloud Connector from the Services menu.

Step 3: Configuring the SAP BTP Connection

  1. To establish a connection between the subaccount you created in SAP BTP and Cloud Connector, go to the Cloud Connector Administration panel and click Add Subaccount.

     

  2. Skip the page requesting host, port, user, and password. Enter the subaccount details from your SAP BTP account.


  3. Navigate to the Cloud To On-Premise tab.
  4. Click the Add System Mapping button and fill in the following information:
    • Backend Type: ABAP System
    • Protocol: HTTPS or HTTP
    • Host: e.g., 10.10.10.10
    • Port: e.g., 8000
  5. Click Save and test the connection.

Mapping Configuration:

  • When creating the system mapping, if you enter '/' in the URL path field, all subdirectories will be included automatically, eliminating the need to specify each subdirectory individually.

With these steps completed, SAP Cloud Connector will be configured and ready for use in integrating your ABAP system with SAP BTP.

 

Establishing a Connection Between BAS Workspace and ABAP System

After creating the connection point for your on-premise system (e.g., TST system) using Cloud Connector, you can establish a connection between your Business Application Studio (BAS) workspace and the system in two ways:

Method 1: Direct Connection from Business Application Studio

  1. In BAS, go to the Service section from the left-hand menu.
  2. Select Add New Service under your account and enter the required details.
  3. After entering the details, click on the service. If a green button appears in the corner, it means the information was entered correctly and the connection is established successfully.

Method 2: Setting Up the Destination in BTP Account

  1. Log in to your BTP account and select your subaccount.
  2. In the left-hand menu, select Connectivity, then choose Destinations.
  3. Before proceeding, click on Cloud Connectors and verify if the access point you created in the Cloud Connector application appears under Exposed Back-End Systems as "available."
  4. Now, from the Destinations section, click Create Destination to create a destination for the service.
  5. Fill in the following details:
    • https://adress:port
    • SAP Client, User, and Password: Provide the credentials for your ABAP backend system.
    • Web IDE System: This should match the information for your ABAP backend system.
    • URL: Enter the URL in the format:
    • Proxy Type: Set to OnPremise.
    • Authentication: Set to BasicAuthentication.
  6. In the Web IDE Usage field, add the following values:
    • odata_abap: For Gateway operations.
    • ui5_execute_abap: To execute UI5 applications from the ABAP repository.
    • dev_abap: For development and deployment operations.
      If additional functionalities are required, you can search for other Web IDE usages by querying WebIDEUsage.
  7. After entering the details, click Check to verify the connection.

     

Once the connection is successfully verified, your BAS workspace will be connected to the ABAP system.

 

Deploying the Application to On-Premise System or Cloud Foundry

After completing the project, open the project directory in the terminal and run the following commands:

  1. npm install
  2. npm build

This will create the node_modules and dist folders.

Then, run the following command:

  • npm run deploy-config
    • or alternatively:
    • npx fiori add deploy-config

At this point, we have two options for deployment: Cloud Foundry or ABAP system.

Option 1: Deploying to Cloud Foundry

  1. When deploying to Cloud Foundry, select CF and it will prompt you for your Cloud Foundry account credentials.
  2. Enter your BTP account email and password. Once entered, it will establish the connection to Cloud Foundry, and if successful, you will be able to deploy the application.
  3. Before proceeding, ensure that the Cloud Foundry service is added and running in your BTP account. When you enter the Cloud Foundry section in your BTP Subaccount, you should see your space with deployed applications.
  4. Additionally, if your application is published as an HTML5 app, it will also appear in the HTML5 Applications section in the left-hand menu of your BTP Subaccount.

Option 2: Deploying to ABAP System

Before starting, create a Transport Request number in your ABAP system.

  1. In BAS, select the ABAP system in the terminal section.
  2. You will then be prompted to choose the destination (the service-destination you added earlier).
  3. Next, you will be asked for the SAPUI5 ABAP Repository. Enter the name of the BSP Application you created under the package.
  4. Afterward, you will be asked for the Deployment Description. Enter your description.
  5. Then, you will be prompted for the Package. Select Enter Manually and provide the name of the package you created.
  6. Finally, input the Transport Request number and complete the process.

Even if the deploy-config is created for the ABAP system, BAS may ask for additional information, and the generated files may not be sufficient to complete the deployment. Therefore, follow these steps:

  1. Open the ui5-deploy.yaml file in your project.
  2. Under the configuration: section, add the following:

credentials:

  username: env:USERNAME

  password: env:PASSWORD

  1. Under the target: section, add:

client: env:CLIENTNUMBER

auth: basic

  1. Save the file.

After making these changes, run npm run deploy in the terminal, and your project will be deployed to the ABAP system with the necessary configurations.

Check SE80 to verify that the project has been successfully deployed.

Lastly, in the SICF menu, enter the name of your BSP application and confirm that the application service is running correctly.

entry.title: Nested Lists on SBPA
entry.content:
Hello, 

On PO item level, in order to upload item text data or account assignment data, we need to bind a list type table in the inputs of the action. Which means, we need to be able to add a list (for item text and account assignment) inside a list (already created for line items on a PO). But on the form, it is not possible to create a nested list ie. list within a list. And the Input section of Post PO action does not provide the ability to do single item binding for these lists. In this case, could you please advise how we bind the data to these type of lists within line item in the inputs section of the action? 

Thank you

Roger

entry.title: SAP Setup for Single Product with Multiple Machine Configurations
entry.content: "We have a single product that is produced on two separate machines, Machine A and Machine B, which have different configurations. Machine A is equipped with 4 filling nozzles, while Machine B is equipped with 16 filling nozzles. The production process and resource utilization, including labor and machine hours, vary depending on the machine used. I would like to know how to configure SAP to accommodate this variability in production and accurately capture the differences in labor and machine hours consumed on each machine."

entry.title: Error Handling with API Gateway vs Service Layer in SAP
entry.content:
When using the Service Layer to post, for example, an order, I receive detailed error messages similar to what is displayed in the SAP Business One (SBO) client if something is not correct.

Now, I’m trying to migrate to the API Gateway to leverage new resources like reports. However, I’ve encountered an issue with error handling. In case of an error, I only receive a "503 Service Unavailable" response code, accompanied by the following response body:

{"code":503,"message": "Internal Server Error resulted from Runtime Exception"}

And it's the same for any endpoint or any kind of error.

How can I get more meaningful error messages when using the API Gateway, similar to what is provided by the Service Layer?

entry.title: Managing Returns with 3 PL and Warehouse operations
entry.content:
Typically, in industries that are based on a model of selling to customers based on their consumption follow a consignment model. The consignment model works on a principle that the customer does not have to purchase all the mix of products upfront, but can keep the products within their promises and only pay for the products that they actually consume. This typically is a requirement for products that may be used in medical procedures where the actual implanted product in a patient is based on what may be required at the time of the procedure.

This presents a requirement for such businesses to ensure that they have all possible combinations provided to the medical facility to support a procedure, invoice the customer for the products used and get back as soon as possible the products that are not required so that they can be used as a source of supply for other customer requirements.

This means a way to be able to quickly receive the products in the warehouse and disposition them so that they can be put back into the supply chain.

SAP's return processing works on the premise that there is a ready to process document in the system when the products are received at the warehouse. If a document is not available, there is a need to create a document first before a return can be processed. Also, SAP expects a different set of documents if a product being returned back is a normal sales return ( using a returns document ) as against a consignment pick up.

In an environment where customers are expected to frequently return back consigned products that are not used, it is a frequent challenge that the products returned back could have been consigned to different locations of a customer. As an example, a hospital chain can have multiple receiving locations, but a centralized location to manage all returns that have to be sent back to the vendors. Most of the products are serialized and carry a unique serial number from a traceability persepctive.

The challenge is that the 3 PL or a warehouse would get a return back, but they have to be able to determine the correct document against which to process the return OR need to have a document created quickly so that the return can be processed. In a high velocity environment, that can present a challenge as the customer service team is typically not within an earshot reach of the warehouse or 3 PL.

What you need is a solution that can allow the 3 PL or warehouse to record the products that have been received and then have an automation solution that would determine if there is an open return document in the system to process the return OR have a new document created so that the return received and processed in the 3 PL can quickly be also transacted in the system.

In the scope of automations that we have, there is very little automation that is done for reverse logistics and a solution framework that allows for a seamless transfer of receipt and records in the SAP system would reduce the need for a manual intervention in such high velocity reverse logistics environments.

entry.title: Credit don't rechecked after modification of the sales order
entry.content:
The customer credit master data was not maintained before the creation of the sales order, and after the customer credit master data was supplemented and the sales order was modified, the credit was still not checked

  1. Initially the customer did not maintain credit master data
  2. Create a sales order, this time the sales order has no credit check
  3. Customer master data Maintain credit master data
  4. Modify the sales order, at this time the credit should be rechecked, but it was not checked

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Credit don't rechecked after modification of the sale...
entry.content: Please follow the resolution steps provided in SAP KBA 3556197 - Credit check is not performed after modification of the sales order .

entry.title: Thank you for another year with SAP University Alliances!
entry.content:
🇲🇽 Spanish Version

Felices Fiestas

Estimado miembro de la Comunidad SAP University Alliances Iberoamérica,

Nos acercamos al cierre de otro año extraordinario y no podríamos estar más agradecidos por todo lo que hemos construido juntos en 2024. Su apoyo, esfuerzo y dedicación han sido esenciales para el éxito de nuestra comunidad. ¡2024 está llegando a su fin, pero nuestra alianza continúa! 

Este año alcanzamos grandes logros: cerramos con un total de 235 instituciones miembro en la región en 21 países y realizamos más de 155 actividades, gracias a su apoyo y al dedicado trabajo de los SAP Next-Gen Ambassadors. Asimismo, queremos expresar nuestro más sincero agradecimiento al Academic Board Ibero-America (ABI), que concluye este ciclo de gestión dejando un legado de compromiso y grandes resultados. Como así también aprovechamos para darle la bienvenida a los integrantes del nuevo ABI que comenzará a ejercer en 2025-2027.

Usted, querido profesor, querida profesora, es una pieza clave para que nuestra misión de transformar el futuro de la educación y preparar a las nuevas generaciones para los desafíos del mercado. Le agradecemos su confianza, valiosa colaboración y creencia en nuestra visión y esperamos seguir contando con su apoyo y entusiasmo en 2025.

Le deseamos un excelente fin de año, lleno de celebraciones junto a las personas que más aprecia. ¡Que 2025 comience con energía renovada y grandes éxitos!

Hasta pronto, ¡felices fiestas y un maravilloso inicio de 2025! 💫

 

🇧🇷Portuguese Version

Boas Festas

Estimado(a) membro(a) da Comunidade SAP University Alliances Ibero-America,

Estamos nos aproximando do encerramento de mais um ano incrível, e não poderíamos estar mais agradecidos por tudo o que construímos juntos em 2024. Seu apoio, esforço e dedicação foram essenciais para o sucesso da nossa comunidade. 2024 está chegando ao fim, mas nossa aliança continua!

Neste ano, alcançamos grandes conquistas: encerramos com um total de 235 instituições-membro na região, em 21 países, e realizamos mais de 155 atividades, graças ao seu apoio e ao dedicado trabalho dos nossos SAP Next-Gen Ambassadors. Além disso, queremos expressar nossa mais sincera gratidão ao Academic Board Ibero-America (ABI), que encerra este ciclo de gestão deixando um legado de compromisso e grandes resultados. Aproveitamos também para dar as boas-vindas aos novos integrantes do ABI, que iniciarão seu mandato de 2025-2027.

Você, querido professor e querida professora, são uma peça fundamental para que possamos cumprir nossa missão de transformar o futuro da educação e preparar as novas gerações para os desafios do mercado. Agradecemos pela sua confiança, valiosa colaboração e por acreditar na nossa visão, e esperamos continuar contando com seu apoio e entusiasmo em 2025.

Desejamos a você um excelente fim de ano, repleto de celebrações ao lado das pessoas que mais aprecia. Que 2025 comece com energia renovada e grandes sucessos!

Até breve, boas festas e um maravilhoso início de 2025! 💫

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @buz 

the source of your routing in xs-app.json is "^odata/(.*)$" which is missing the leading / (compare to other routes.
Possibly this causes that the route is not matched and not used at all.
What alerts me is the setting "csrfProtection": false. This is very dangerous as it opens your app for CSRF attacks. It is crucial that approuter performs csrf protection as it is the entity that receives a session cookie which could be misused otherwise. Usually a backend security filter will not do CSRF protection, when the authentication is based on a jwt token. Please consider turning it on again.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors WorkZone Page Load Times
entry.content: We are in the process of implementing SAP SuccessFactors WorkZone to replace our portal. We are using it as the central hub for our users to access services and applications from SuccessFactors and our on-premise SAP ERP system using the Cloud Connector. We are finding, however, that the page load times are relatively lengthy. The home page is taking 6 to 7 seconds to load and when opening applications we are seeing a slow load time. Fiori apps and SF tiles do a bit better. Has anyone had success or figured out any tricks to getting the page load times and responsiveness to increase?

entry.title: Portuguese National Budget for 2025- Payroll Legal Changes
entry.content:
Happy New Year 2025 to you All.

As we embrace this festive season, we wish you a prosperous New Year in 2025.

It's a time for celebration and joy, and we want to take this opportunity to share some important updates regarding the upcoming State Budget for 2025, which will bring changes to the salary system.

Before diving into the details, here's what you need to know first:

We would like to share with you the upcoming changes regarding the State Budget for 2025, which will impact the salary system. But first, let us identify what is most important:

  • Minimum Support Package Level for implementing the new updates of the 2025 State Budget:

Release

Support Package

S4HCM 101

HR SP 00

S4HCM 100

HR SP 04

SAP_HR 6.00

HR SP M1

SAP_HR 6.04

HR SP I7

SAP_HR 6.08

HR SP B5

As you all know, it is necessary to have these Support Packages at a minimum in order to install the notes we plan to publish in January with the solutions.

We are focused on delivering a solution for the following scenarios:

  • Exempt Lunch Allowance amount for 2025 (Limite de isenção Subsídio de Refeição em senhas para 2025)
  • Changes of Index dos Socials Support (IAS) constant for 2025 (alteração do Indexante dos Apoios Sociais 2025)
  • Minimun Wage for Portugal (mainland) and Azores Islands 2025 (Salário Minimo do Continente e Açores 2025)
  • Minimum Wage for Madeira Islands 2025
  • IRS Incentive to Hire Young People 2025 (Incentivo de IRS Jovens 2025)
  • Overtime for residents – New tax autonomous rate for all the number of hours (novo cálculo de de taxa de IRS Trabalho Suplementar 2025) 
  • Overtime for non residents – New number of hours of tax exempt, from limit of 50 hours to the limit of 100 hours of overtime (Novo cálculo de limite de horas de isenção de IRS Trabalho Suplementar não residentes) 

Additional scenarios may arise following the publication of the State Budget Law. This planning summary is being made based on the text of the proposed State Budget Law for 2025.

We would thus like to highlight the expected changes for the Young People IRS incentive:

  • Consideration of 10 years of applicability instead of 5 years
  • The monthly benefit limit will be the same for all years (55xIAS/14)
  • We present the planned table for this benefit:

Year

Benefit (%)

Monthly Limit Formula

Monthly exemption limit amount

1

100

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

2

75

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

3

75

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

4

75

55 * IAS / 14

55 * IAS / 14

2000,66

Regarding the taxation (tax retention) of overtime, according to the proposed law, paragraph 8 of Article 99-C of the CIRS will be amended as follows (in bold):

“8- When remuneration for overtime work is paid, the withholding tax rate to be applied is 50% of the rate applicable to the dependent work monthly remuneration for the month in which the overtime is paid or made available.”

Paragraph 10 of the same article will be repealed :

“10 - The autonomous withholding tax rate applied to remuneration for overtime work, as provided in paragraphs 5 and 8, is reduced by 50% from the 101st hour onward, inclusive.”

These changes are expected to simplify the IRS calculations for overtime work that were introduced in 2023, making the process more streamlined and efficient.

Regarding Overtime for non residents – New number of hours of tax exempt (from limit of 50 hours to the limit of 100 hours of overtime) with the maximum exempt amount that was already set of RMMG (€870,00).

We hope you find these updates helpful as we move into the new year. Thank you for your attention, and here's to a remarkable 2025!

entry.title: Re: Counter Routing more than 99 in Manage Routings / Cre...
entry.content:
The field Group counter (PLKO-PLNAL) is a char 2 field so can only contain a numeric value up to 99. However, the two-digit field is alpha-numeric so there is larger range available.

Enter an alpha-numeric value such as "N1" for more than 99 entries

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content:
Regarding the paging issue:

How to implement paging for a custom entity is described in https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/requesting-and-setting-data-or-count-in-unmanaged-query?version=Cloud&locale=en-US
There the following note is given: "If data is requested, the method set_data must be called. If the total number of records is requested, the method set_total_number_of_records must be called. Otherwise there will be an error during runtime. The io_response->set_data( ) method is only allowed to fill elements listed by io_request->get_requested_elements( ). System may react with a failure or a short dump if these rules aren't followed. In UI scenarios with Fiori Elements the total number of records is always requested by the UI."

Regarding the available CDS views:

The field type "Code List based on CDS View" and also the restrictions regarding available CDS views is explained here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/acb2907f362944a5a9e1b5eb86e769dc.html

One can sum up the restrictions as follows: "You can either select a CDS-based view (Custom CDS View/SAP delivered CDS view released according to the C1 contract), or you can select a custom entity."

entry.title: Amortization Model in SAC
entry.content:
I am working on an amortization model for my project in SAC, and I am facing the following issue:

I have an Excel dataset with the following information:

  • Asset type (e.g., A, B, C, etc.)
  • Useful life (in months)
  • Acquisition value
  • Acquisition date
  • Start of payment date

What I would like to implement in SAC is:

  1. A table showing:
    • The start of payment date.
    • The monthly payments over the asset's useful life.
  2. For each row in the table, the monthly payment value would be calculated as:
    Acquisition value / Useful life.

Practical example:

  • Asset type: A
  • Useful life: 6 months
  • Acquisition value: 5000
  • Acquisition date: 2024-01 (January 2024)
  • Start of payment date: 2024-02 (February 2024)

In the table, the payments would appear as follows:

  • February 2024: 833.33
  • March 2024: 833.33
  • April 2024: 833.33
  • May 2024: 833.33
  • June 2024: 833.33
  • July 2024: 833.33

In other words, the total acquisition value is divided by the useful life and proportionally distributed across the corresponding months. I need help understanding how to implement this model in SAC so that the table is generated correctly.

entry.title: Maximizing BTP Job Scheduling Service with SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model
entry.content:
Hi SAP PAPM UM Community, 

Before I start my blogpost, I would like to appreciate your commitment in learning and understanding SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud, Universal Model (SAP PAPM Cloud UM). With such interest, our team is getting fueled with writing more and more blogposts that hopefully can help you in your journey with SAP PAPM Cloud UM.

ℹ️ INFORMATION
If you are new to the blogpost series, please visit this site for more interesting blogposts 

Today as my final entry for the year 2024, I would like to share with you a trick on how you can leverage some actions such as Scheduled Activity Execution with the help of SAP Job Scheduling Service (SAP JSS). With this technique you can then schedule your Activity execution for a one-time purpose or if need be periodically. 

Just to organize our thoughts, let us discuss this topic into phases by setting up

Phase I. SAP BTP: Application and Instance Subscriptions
Phase II. SAP BTP: Creation of BTP Customized Application
Phase III. SAP PAPM Cloud UM: Collect Activity API (Action URL)
Phase IV. SAP JSS: Job Creation
Phase V. SAP JSS: Job Scheduling
Phase VI. SAP PAPM Cloud UM & SAP JSS: Run validation 

 

⚠️ WARNING
Assumption is you already have an active SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model Application with Environment and Activities. 

Let us proceed.

 

Phase I - SAP BTP: Application and Instance Subscriptions

1) Subaccount Entitlement

In case you are only subscribed to SAP PAPM Cloud Universal Model Application and not to the other services needed then you need to do entitlement for below services. 

ServicePurpose
SAP PAPM Cloud UM
Plan: Default Service Instance

This will be used to bind the custom application that we are to create later in Phase II to SAP PAPM Cloud UM.

⚠️ SAP Job Scheduling Service is not allowing credential entry, so we will use a BTP created application to bridge SAP JSS and SAP PAPM Cloud UM. You will know more about this in Phase 2.

 

Cloud Foundry Runtime
Plan: Memory

This is necessary in order for us to deploy or create a custom application in our subaccount. 

Authorization and Trust Management Service
Plan: Application 

This is a prerequisite of Job Scheduler which helps in binding the custom application that we are to create later in Phase II to SAP Job Scheduling Service

Job Scheduling Service
Plan: Standard

Will be used to schedule the activity run either as one time execution or periodic. 

 

2) Cloud Foundry Activation

If your subaccount is on AWS EU10, ensure to activate cloud foundry in landscape cf-eu10-004. Custom Application creation (for our Phase II) in cf-eu10 landscape is not permitted by BTP due to memory restriction. 

 

3) Cloud Foundry Space Creation

Create a space where the services and the custom application will be created. 

 

4) SAP PaPM Cloud Service Instance and Service Key Creation

To create SAP PaPM Cloud Service Instance follow the official help documentation .
Ensure to also use the scope provided by the guide in order to get proper access to SAP PaPM Cloud Applications. 

 

5) Job Scheduling Service Instance Creation

Follow steps 1-5 of the following official help guide to create a job scheduling service instance. Ignore for now step 6, that will be performed in the next phase. 

⚠️ WARNING
It is important to remember the Job Scheduling Service Instance Name -- in this case I named it jss. This instance name is to be used as part of the XSUAA's scope definition. 

 

6) XSUAA Service Creation with SAP JSS Scopes

As a security prerequisite of Job Scheduling Service, Standard Plan; an Authorization and Trust Management Service must be created with the SAP JSS scopes. Inspired by the scope provided by the SAP JSS official documentation, we created below that you can also copy upon XSUAA instance creation. 

 

{
  "xsappname": "jba-application",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Access",
      "grant-as-authority-to-apps": ["$XSSERVICENAME(<jobscheduler instance name>)"]
    }
  ],
  "authorities": ["$XSAPPNAME.Access"],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "Access",
      "scope-references": ["$XSAPPNAME.Access"]
    }
  ]
}

 

 

Now that we have all the services necessary, let us proceed with Phase II and create our Application. 

 

Phase II - SAP BTP: Creation of Customized Application

Since SAP Job Scheduling Service is not allowing a direct ClientID, ClientSecret (or credential) input, we will create an application that will serve as a proxy to create token needed by the jobscheduler to avoid authentication issue upon calling SAP PAPM Cloud UM Application or Activity like below. 

With the help of a developer teammate, we have created below SAMPLE custom files that you may also use as inspiration in creating your custom proxy application (of course you are free to adjust it and make it more sophisticated)

1) package.json

 

{
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/xsenv": "^5.4.0",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.2.7",
    "axios": "^1.7.9",
    "express": "^4.21.2",
    "http-proxy-middleware": "^3.0.3",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^18",
    "npm": "^9"
  }
}

 

 

2) manifest.yml

change line

  • 3 with the application name that you want to use.
  • 7 with your application route
  • 10 with your xsuaa service name
  • 11 with your papm service name
  • 13 & 14 with your technical user's credentials

 

---
applications:
- name: jobscheduler-papm-proxy2
  buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  command: node index.js
  routes:
    - route: jobscheduler-papm-proxy2.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com
  services:
    #servicenames of your xsuaa and papm service
    - xsuaaservice 
    - papmservice
  env:
    BTP_USERNAME: "<change with your technical id>"
    BTP_PASSWORD: "<password of your techincal id>"

 

 

3) index.js

change line

  • 11 with label: "<xsuaa service name>"
  • 87 with label: "<papm service name>"

 

const express = require("express");
const axios = require("axios");
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
const { stringify } = require("querystring");
const { serviceCredentials } = require("@sap/xsenv");
const { createSecurityContext, XsuaaService } = require("@sap/xssec");
const { createProxyMiddleware, errorResponsePlugin } = require("http-proxy-middleware");

const app = express();

const xsuaaService = serviceCredentials({ label: "xsuaaservice" });
const authService = new XsuaaService(xsuaaService);
const authMiddleware = async (req, res, next) => {
  try {
    req.securityContext = await createSecurityContext(authService, { req });
    return next();
  } catch (e) {
    return res.status(401).json(e);
  }
};

app.get("/", (req, res) => res.send("OK"));

app.use(authMiddleware);
app.get("/authentication", (req, res) =>
  res.send(req.securityContext.token.userId)
);

const scopeMiddleware = (req, res, next) => {
  if (req.securityContext.checkLocalScope("Access")) {
    return next();
  } else {
    res.sendStatus(403);
  }
};

app.use(scopeMiddleware);
app.get("/authorization", (req, res) =>
  res.json(req.securityContext.token.scopes)
);

class RequestService {
  constructor(credentials) {
    this.credentials = credentials;
    this.cache = null;
  }
  async getOAuthToken(grant_type = "client_credentials", parameters = {}) {
    if (this.isValidToken()) return this.getCache();
    const { clientid, clientsecret, url } = this.credentials.uaa;
    const data = stringify(parameters);
    try {
      const response = await axios.post(
        `${url}/oauth/token?grant_type=${grant_type}`,
        data,
        {
          headers: data ? { 'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' } : {},
          auth: {
            username: clientid,
            password: clientsecret,
          },
        }
      );
      this.setCache(response.data);
      return response.data;
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(err);
    }
  }
  isValidToken() {
    const data = this.getCache();
    if (!data) return false;
    const now = Date.now();
    const decoded = jwt.decode(data.access_token);
    if (!decoded) return false;
    const remainingTime = (Number(decoded.exp) * 1000 - now) / (1000 * 60);
    if (remainingTime < 30) return false;
    return true;
  }
  setCache(value) {
    this.cache = value;
  }
  getCache() {
    return this.cache;
  }
}

const papmService = serviceCredentials({ label: "papmservice" });
const username = process.env.BTP_USERNAME;
const password = process.env.BTP_PASSWORD;
const grant_type = username && password ? "password" : "client_credentials";
const parameters = username && password ? { username, password } : {};
const requestService = new RequestService(papmService);

const proxyMiddleware = createProxyMiddleware({
  target: `https://${papmService.url}`,
  changeOrigin: true,
  pathRewrite: async (path, req) => {
    const token = await requestService.getOAuthToken(grant_type, parameters);
    req.headers.authorization = `Bearer ${token.access_token}`;
    return path;
  },
  on: {
    proxyReq: (proxyReq, req) => {
      const { method, path, headers = proxyReq.getHeaders() } = proxyReq;
      console.log(`Proxy sending request: ${method} ${path}; ${Object.entries(headers).map(([k, v]) => `${k}: ${v}`).join("; ")}`);
    },
    proxyRes: (proxyRes, req) => {
      const { statusCode, statusMessage, req: { _header } } = proxyRes;
      console.log(`Proxy receiving response: ${_header.trim().split(/\r\n/).join("; ")} - ${statusCode} ${statusMessage}`);
    },
    error: (error, req, res, target) => {
      console.log(`Proxy error: ${error.toString()}, on target: ${target && target.href}`);
    },
  },
  plugins: [errorResponsePlugin],
});
app.use(proxyMiddleware);

const port = process.env.PORT ?? 3000;
app.listen(port);

 

 

4) Create a folder with the files and a zip version

 

5) Deploy the Custom Application (proxy)

 

6) Check the Application's Service Binding

It is important to see om the Service Bindings section "automatically" both PaPM and XSUAA services

 

7) Ensure to assign the technical user with proper role (collections)

It is important to assign the technical user (declared in the manifest.yml) with proper SAP PAPM Cloud UM roles as well as the "Access"  role generated by the deployed custom proxy application.

 

Now that we are all set with Phases I & II, I will now teach you how to collect or take note of the SAP PAPM Cloud UM API URL to serve as your action URL when setting up a SAP JSS job. 

 

Phase III - SAP PAPM Cloud UM: Collect activity API (Action URL)

Assuming that you already have a PAPM Cloud UM Activity that is being wanted to be scheduled for execution via SAP Job Scheduling Service. It is then very important to get the action URL or Activity API URL that can be fed to SAP JSS. The format would be

<BTP Proxy Application Routes>/ace/<$batch's request url>/<$batch's payload>
Something like

https://jobscheduler-papm-proxy.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ace/sap/ace/ProcessService/Activities(ID='422e06bf-abc2-46a2-ac32-db7cefbf0fc0',IsActiveEntity=true)/sap.ace.ProcessService.runActivity
 

ℹ️INFORMATION
You need to enable the Developer Tools on your browser to be guided with the accurate $batch request URL and payload

 

The original $batch information could be collected upon choosing or pressing an action such as activity "Run". Make sure that you change the first few parts of the request URL with the proxy application route we created in Phase II.  

 

Phase IV - SAP JSS: Job Creation

Now that we have all the information we need, we are then ready to create a job in SAP Job Scheduling Service.

ℹ️ INFORMATION
SAP Job Scheduling Service uses UTC (that is -1 CET Time). 

 

Phase V - SAP JSS: Job Scheduling

Using the official guide, you will see that there are atleast 3 types of schedule Formats in SAP JSS such as Cron, Date/Time, and Human-Readable Date Formats. I am personally more comfortable with cron hence will be giving my example in cron form. Feel free to check which option(s) is more comfortable for you.

 

ℹ️ INFORMATION
SAP Job Scheduling Service uses UTC (that is -1 CET Time). Also it is important to know that the {} in the Data (JSON) is important for the scheduler to execute SAP PAPM UM Activities using the Custom Proxy Application I provided in Phase II.  

1) One-Time Run

 

2) Scheduled to run every 5 minutes (via cron)

The job that I will schedule means that it will run every 5minutes "From" the time set until the time "To". If you are interested to know more how to handle cron format, visit the official documentation.

I will use * * * * * */5 0
>> this means, for any year, month, day, day of the week, hour, it will run every 5minutes at exactly 0 second. 

 

Now that we have scheduled the run, let us validate that the run was successful by checking both SAP PAPM UM (event logs) and SAP JSS (Run logs)

 

Phase VI - SAP PAPM Cloud UM & SAP JSS: Run Validation

1) SAP JSS

You can see in the Run Log that the job is executing every 5 minutes successfully from 16:05 UTC. 

 

2) SAP PaPM Cloud UM

You can see in SAP PAPM Cloud UM's Event Logs that the runs were also successful at exactly 17:05 - onwards every 5 minutes of my local time, CET Time. 

 

Success!!! ☁️ ⏰ 🔄

Alright, that is all for now! I hope this excites you and helps you moving forward with your job scheduling agenda with SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model.

Have a pleasant year end, and see you next year in our 2025 blogpost entries!

entry.title: Re: Unable to Update manually added Standard Fields to St...
entry.content:
Hello @Sanket001,

to add a standard field on the standard API just in output (GET) you can create an Extension view (you can find a sample on the prevoius post) just adding the field you need. In my case this worked properly.
To update a stardard field using API (POST/PATCH/PUT), my approach was to create a custom field, copy of the standard field, using SAP Fiori Extensibility ->Custom Fields and using an exit to move the value of the custom field on the standard field.
If you need more information, don't hesitate to contact me 😉

Best regards,
F.

entry.title: Re: Running Year to Date (YTD) on fiscal periods with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@MariannevL 

1) Are you really sure that the execution plan do not "fallback to older engines" ?

from what I see HEX uses way less memory than JOIN/OLAP/CALC engines.

2) Non equi join are killer to HANA. With HEX they introduce specific phisical operators to deal with non equi join ("range joins"), while the older engines can not do this kind of join efficiently => is not that HEX sucks in equi-join, rather than HEX works better in non-equi-join in comparison to the older engines. from what I see HEX suffer a lot from cast and trim on join column, more than the previous engines, but in general it works better il 99% of the time.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Purchase Order Create while transfer Shipment Cost.
entry.content:
Hi Moazzam,

Please, could you share me a new link?.

Is it possible  to disable "NO LIMIT" field in PO when is created automatically and configure overall Limit in the PO?

Thank you for your help

entry.title: Re: Running QTD, YTD in SAP Datasphere using SQL View
entry.content:
Did the model even suggest that when during a fiscal period there are no posting on one particular account, with the window function the cumulated value for that account do not appear in that specific period? (=> wrong totals)

Since LLM are trained "generally", while the query optimization depends heavily on the specific DBMS it is executed, are you really sure that the query given is the optimal for HANA that is an in memory column store database ? (did you test it with PlanViz/ SQL Analyzer? the last time I check performances were the same)

"using window functions is more efficient than using subqueries or self-joins":

- is totally not trivial as untill like yesterday window function were executed in ROW engine leading to OOM issues due to row format Itab.

- range joins operators were added to HEX to improve non equi joins performances.

entry.title: Re: Running a Jupyter notebook in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: Struggled with this for a long time, using BAS found that PIP INSTALL puts the packages in a different place than Jupyter notebook is looking for them. Had to add destination using SYS.PATH.APPEND and it was necessary to user IMPORT SYS first. Had to find the path first using "CD /HOME/" from the terminal then navigating down, /USER/.ASDF-INST/INSTALLS/PYTHON/3.11.2/LIB/PYTHON3.11/SITE-PACKAGES. Would be interested to know if there's a better way to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service - Part 1
entry.content:
I found the solution, I had to add the destination at subaccount level and not at instance level:

subaccount:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
            HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
            Name: RoyCustDeclaration-srv-api
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
          existing_destinations_policy: update

thank you also for the hint about the "csrfProtection" issue.

Ciao ciao!

 

Andrea

entry.title: MATDOC IS getting in message for NRGP =1 BUT NOT FOR NRGP =2
entry.content:
MODULE USER_COMMAND INPUT.

GV_UCOMM = SY-UCOMM.
DATA : LV_REQ TYPE ZREQ.
DATA : LV_IREQ TYPE ZIREQ.
DATA : LV_INTER(10) TYPE N."zinter.
DATA : GJAHR(8) TYPE C.
DATA: LV_ZTAG1 TYPE T052-ZTAG1,
LV_ZTERM TYPE LFM1-ZTERM.


CLEAR: wa_gmitem, wa_gmhead,matdoc, it_gmitem.

IF GV_UCOMM = '&SAVE'.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB
IF CUST EQ 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-KUNNR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
ENDIF.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB

IF ( "zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-werks IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lgort IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lifnr IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-nrgp IS INITIAL ).

MESSAGE: 'Please enter No of Packages, Plant, Stor. Loc., Vendor, RGP'
TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT EQ '00000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT = SY-DATUM.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET EQ '000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET = SY-UZEIT.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH EQ SPACE.
zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH = SY-UNAME.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = SPACE.
SELECT MAX( REQ ) INTO LV_REQ FROM zsc_head_sadm2.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = LV_REQ + 1.
* zsc_head_sadm2-rdate = sy-datum.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '1' AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS INITIAL.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
*************************************************************
LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @DATA(LV_MTART)
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

* IF it_return IS NOT INITIAL.
IF it_return IS INITIAL. """"Added by Akshata
DATA(lv_check) = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

*************************************************************
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' AND lv_check = abap_true.
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'J' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
"""""""Begin of Changes by Eidikos Mahesh K""""""""
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
UPDATE zsc_head_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
CCREADATE = SY-DATUM
CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.
zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ELSEIF ( zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '2'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '3'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '4' )
AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS NOT INITIAL.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'. """"""ADDED BY AKSHATA
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @LV_MTART
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF it_return IS INITIAL.
lv_check = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE """added by akshata z41 to z61
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' and lv_check = abap_true. """added by akshata
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'O' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
zsc_head_sadm2-remarks = zsc_head_sadm2-remarks.
zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg = zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg.

* UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
* WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
* AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.

zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WA_GMHEAD-REF_DOC_NO = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.
WA_LINE-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ NE '0000'.
VAR = WA_LINE-IREQ.
ENDIF.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ EQ '0000'.
VAR = VAR + 1.
WA_LINE-IREQ = VAR.
ENDIF.
WA_LINE-KWERT = WA_LINE-MENGE * WA_LINE-KBETR.
WA_LINE-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
WA_LINE-GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
WA_LINE-REMQTY = WA_LINE-MENGE.

**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

MODIFY it_line FROM wa_line TRANSPORTING kwert
inter
gjahr
remqty.
* MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM WA_LINE.

CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF MATDOC IS NOT INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z41'.

ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSE.
* READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
* IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* SEPARATED BY SPACE.
* MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
* ENDIF.
* ENDIF.

""""""""""""""""""""""Added by Akshata 26.12.2024""""""""""'
ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

""""""""""""ENd of chnages 26.12.2024""""""""""""""""""""""

DATA : MESS TYPE CHAR200.

IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'. "AND matdoc IS NOT INITIAL.
CONCATENATE 'Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-REQ 'AND'
'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
'AND' 'Material Document :' matdoc
'has been saved.'
INTO MESS
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG BP SOA - No confirmation message available wh...
entry.content: @Louis : Please let me know what solution you have implemented for this requirement, I too have similar requirement

entry.title: MATDOC IS GEETING IN MESSAG EFOR NRGP =1 BUT NOT FOR NRGP=2 ..
entry.content:
MODULE USER_COMMAND INPUT.

GV_UCOMM = SY-UCOMM.
DATA : LV_REQ TYPE ZREQ.
DATA : LV_IREQ TYPE ZIREQ.
DATA : LV_INTER(10) TYPE N."zinter.
DATA : GJAHR(8) TYPE C.
DATA: LV_ZTAG1 TYPE T052-ZTAG1,
LV_ZTERM TYPE LFM1-ZTERM.


CLEAR: wa_gmitem, wa_gmhead,matdoc, it_gmitem.

IF GV_UCOMM = '&SAVE'.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB
IF CUST EQ 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-KUNNR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
ENDIF.
* *ADDED BY PRATIKB

IF ( "zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-werks IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lgort IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-lifnr IS INITIAL OR
zsc_head_sadm2-nrgp IS INITIAL ).

MESSAGE: 'Please enter No of Packages, Plant, Stor. Loc., Vendor, RGP'
TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP NE SPACE.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT EQ '00000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERDAT = SY-DATUM.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET EQ '000000'.
zsc_head_sadm2-ERZET = SY-UZEIT.
ENDIF.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH EQ SPACE.
zsc_head_sadm2-AUTH = SY-UNAME.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = SPACE.
SELECT MAX( REQ ) INTO LV_REQ FROM zsc_head_sadm2.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = LV_REQ + 1.
* zsc_head_sadm2-rdate = sy-datum.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
ENDIF.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '1' AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS INITIAL.
IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
*************************************************************
LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @DATA(LV_MTART)
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

* IF it_return IS NOT INITIAL.
IF it_return IS INITIAL. """"Added by Akshata
DATA(lv_check) = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

*************************************************************
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' AND lv_check = abap_true.
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'J' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
"""""""Begin of Changes by Eidikos Mahesh K""""""""
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
UPDATE zsc_head_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
CCREADATE = SY-DATUM
CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.
zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ELSEIF ( zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '2'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '3'
OR zsc_head_sadm2-NRGP = '4' )
AND zsc_head_sadm2-KOSTL IS NOT INITIAL.

IF zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = SPACE.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.

WA_GMHEAD-PSTNG_DATE = SY-DATUM.
WA_GMHEAD-DOC_DATE = SY-DATUM.

WA_GMITEM-MATERIAL = WA_LINE-MATNR.
WA_GMITEM-PLANT = zsc_head_sadm2-WERKS.
WA_GMITEM-STGE_LOC = zsc_head_sadm2-LGORT.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'.

IF CUST = 'X'.
WA_GMITEM-CUSTOMER = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
* wa_gmitem-spec_stock = 'W'.
* WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
WA_GMITEM-VENDOR = zsc_head_sadm2-LIFNR.
WA_GMITEM-MOVE_TYPE = 'Z61'. """"""ADDED BY AKSHATA
ENDIF.

WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_QNT = WA_LINE-MENGE.
WA_GMITEM-ENTRY_UOM = WA_LINE-MEINS.

SELECT SINGLE MTART FROM MARA
INTO @LV_MTART
WHERE MATNR = @wa_LINE-MATNR.

IF LV_MTART <> 'UNBW'.
WA_GMITEM-AMOUNT_LC = WA_LINE-KWERT.
ENDIF.

APPEND WA_GMITEM TO IT_GMITEM.
CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
TESTRUN = 'X'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF it_return IS INITIAL.
lv_check = abap_true.
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE """added by akshata z41 to z61
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

**-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC' and lv_check = abap_true. """added by akshata
CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
NR_RANGE_NR = 'Q2'
OBJECT = 'ZNRGP'
* quantity = '000000001'
IMPORTING
NUMBER = LV_INTER
EXCEPTIONS
INTERVAL_NOT_FOUND = 1
NUMBER_RANGE_NOT_INTERN = 2
OBJECT_NOT_FOUND = 3
QUANTITY_IS_0 = 4
QUANTITY_IS_NOT_1 = 5
INTERVAL_OVERFLOW = 6
BUFFER_OVERFLOW = 7
OTHERS = 8.
CONCATENATE 'O' LV_INTER INTO zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREADATE = SY-DATUM.
zsc_head_sadm2-CCREATIME = SY-UZEIT.
zsc_head_sadm2-remarks = zsc_head_sadm2-remarks.
zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg = zsc_head_sadm2-nopckg.

* UPDATE zsc_line_sadm2 SET INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
* WHERE REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ
* AND GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'GM_GET_FISCAL_YEAR'
EXPORTING
I_DATE = SY-DATUM
I_FYV = 'V3'
IMPORTING
E_FY = GJAHR.

zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR = GJAHR.
CLEAR : LV_INTER, GJAHR.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WA_GMHEAD-REF_DOC_NO = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.

LOOP AT IT_LINE INTO WA_LINE WHERE MATNR IS NOT INITIAL.
WA_LINE-REQ = zsc_head_sadm2-REQ.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ NE '0000'.
VAR = WA_LINE-IREQ.
ENDIF.
IF WA_LINE-IREQ EQ '0000'.
VAR = VAR + 1.
WA_LINE-IREQ = VAR.
ENDIF.
WA_LINE-KWERT = WA_LINE-MENGE * WA_LINE-KBETR.
WA_LINE-INTER = zsc_head_sadm2-INTER.
WA_LINE-GJAHR = zsc_head_sadm2-GJAHR.
WA_LINE-REMQTY = WA_LINE-MENGE.

**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024

MODIFY it_line FROM wa_line TRANSPORTING kwert
inter
gjahr
remqty.
* MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM WA_LINE.

CLEAR : WA_LINE, WA_GMITEM.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
GOODSMVT_HEADER = WA_GMHEAD
GOODSMVT_CODE = '03'
IMPORTING
MATERIALDOCUMENT = MATDOC
TABLES
GOODSMVT_ITEM = IT_GMITEM
RETURN = IT_RETURN.

IF MATDOC IS NOT INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z41'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z31'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z41'.

ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSE.
* READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
* IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
* CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z41' INTO MESSAGE
* SEPARATED BY SPACE.
* MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
* ENDIF.
* ENDIF.

""""""""""""""""""""""Added by Akshata 26.12.2024""""""""""'
ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
WAIT = ABAP_TRUE.

zsc_head_sadm2-DCMBLNR = MATDOC.
IF CUST = 'X'.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
zsc_head_sadm2-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.

MODIFY zsc_head_sadm2 FROM zsc_head_sadm2.

LOOP AT it_line INTO wa_line.
wa_line-req = zsc_head_sadm2-req.
wa_line-dcmblnr = matdoc.
IF CUST = 'X'.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ELSE.
wa_line-DCBWART = 'Z61'.
ENDIF.
* wa_line-dcbwart = 'Z41'.

MODIFY zsc_line_sadm2 FROM wa_line.

COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
CLEAR: wa_line..
ENDLOOP.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
* ELSEIF MATDOC IS INITIAL.
* DATA : mess TYPE char200.
* IF sy-tcode EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'.
* CONCATENATE 'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-inter
** 'And Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-req
* 'has been saved.'
* INTO mess
* SEPARATED BY space.
* ENDIF.
**------------added by sadmabap(PNB) 16.10.2024
ELSE.
READ TABLE IT_RETURN INTO WA_RETURN WITH KEY TYPE = 'E'.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
CONCATENATE WA_RETURN-MESSAGE 'for Movement Type Z61' INTO MESSAGE
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
MESSAGE: MESSAGE TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

""""""""""""ENd of chnages 26.12.2024""""""""""""""""""""""

DATA : MESS TYPE CHAR200.

IF SY-TCODE EQ 'ZSADM_SUBCDC'. "AND matdoc IS NOT INITIAL.
CONCATENATE 'Requset No' zsc_head_sadm2-REQ 'AND'
'Challan No : ' zsc_head_sadm2-INTER
'AND' 'Material Document :' matdoc
'has been saved.'
INTO MESS
SEPARATED BY SPACE.
ENDIF.
